name: Nautilus
playstyleSummary: Nautilus is a tank who initiates fights by hooking an enemy from range and then chaining crowd control to lock them down for his team. He feels methodical rather than fast - you're patiently waiting for a good hook, then following up hard once it lands.
strengths: Dredge Line is a strong long-range hook that starts fights
Very tanky, with a shield that adds area damage over time once active
Riptide provides a reliable area slow around himself
Depth Charge is a near-guaranteed knock-up and stun once it locks on
weaknesses: Relatively slow and immobile outside of his hook
Mana hungry early in the game
Low personal damage compared to true damage-focused champions
His telegraphed engage can be interrupted or crowd controlled before it lands

abilities.name: Staggering Blow
abilities.howToUseIt: Your first attack on a target deals bonus damage and briefly roots them - use it to guarantee a follow-up before they can walk away.

abilities.name: Dredge Line
abilities.howToUseIt: Aim this at terrain to pull yourself toward an enemy, or directly at an enemy to pull them to you - this is your main engage tool, so look for a good angle before committing.

abilities.name: Titan's Wrath
abilities.howToUseIt: Build up the shield before engaging so your attacks are already dealing extra damage over time to everyone nearby once you arrive.

abilities.name: Riptide
abilities.howToUseIt: Use this around yourself when enemies are chasing or fighting you - the explosions are delayed, so it also works as a deterrent against enemies following you.

abilities.name: Depth Charge
abilities.howToUseIt: Fire this at a target during a fight for a near-guaranteed knock-up and stun once it reaches them - use it as a follow-up right after your hook lands.

earlyGamePlan: Look for chances to hook an enemy with Dredge Line, ideally off terrain for a longer pull, to set up a kill with your lane partner. Play a little cautiously otherwise, since you're mana hungry early and don't have much damage without landing your hook.
quickTips: Aim Dredge Line at terrain near an enemy for a sneaky pull, since it's harder to predict than aiming directly at them.
Follow up a landed hook with Depth Charge for a guaranteed knock-up and stun.
Build Titan's Wrath's shield before engaging so you're already dealing bonus damage the moment you arrive.
Riptide's explosions are delayed, so use it defensively while retreating - enemies chasing you will walk into it.
Watch your mana early, since your engage tools all cost resources you can't always refill quickly.
whatToExpect: Nautilus will try to hook you with Dredge Line to pull you in and lock you down, often finishing with Depth Charge for a guaranteed stun. Stay behind minions or terrain to block his hook, and if he does land it, expect heavy crowd control to follow - use your own defensive tools immediately rather than trying to fight through it.

glossary.definition: Starting a fight, usually by committing crowd control or a dash onto an enemy.
A targeted crowd control ability that pulls an enemy toward the caster.
Effects like stuns, roots, or slows that limit an enemy's ability to move or act.
Additional damage or crowd control used right after an initial ability lands.
Something that discourages enemies from approaching or chasing.
Needing to spend a lot of mana relative to how much is available, leading to resource shortages.
